question: Which rapper released the album 'The Marshall Mathers LP' in 2000?
funFact: The Marshall Mathers LP sold around 1.76 million copies in its first week, setting a record at the time for a solo artist.

question: Which group released the landmark 1988 album 'Straight Outta Compton'?
funFact: The album's track 'F*** tha Police' prompted an FBI assistant director to send a warning letter to the group's record label in 1989.

question: Which rapper is known by the nickname 'Hov'?
funFact: In 2019, Forbes declared Jay-Z hip-hop's first billionaire, thanks to ventures like Roc Nation, Armand de Brignac champagne, and D'Ussé cognac.

question: 'California Love' featuring Dr. Dre was a massive 1995 hit for which rapper?
funFact: 'California Love' was 2Pac's first single after his release from prison, recorded almost immediately after he signed with Death Row Records.

question: Method Man is a member of which hip-hop group?
funFact: Wu-Tang Clan hails from Staten Island, New York, which the group famously rebranded as 'Shaolin' in their kung fu-inspired mythology.

question: Which artist released the 2015 hit 'Hotline Bling'?
funFact: 'Hotline Bling' won Best Rap Song and Best Rap/Sung Performance at the 2017 Grammys, even though Drake himself pointed out it isn't really a rap song.

question: What was The Notorious B.I.G.'s debut studio album?
funFact: 'Ready to Die' (1994) was the only studio album Biggie released in his lifetime; 'Life After Death' came out just 16 days after his murder in 1997.

question: Which female rapper released the hit single 'Super Bass' in 2011?
funFact: 'Super Bass' has been certified Diamond by the RIAA, making it one of the best-selling singles ever by a female rapper.

question: What was the title of Snoop Dogg's 1993 debut album, produced by Dr. Dre?
funFact: Doggystyle debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard 200 with over 800,000 copies sold in its first week, then the biggest debut ever for a new artist.

question: In which city was hip-hop born?
funFact: Hip-hop's birth is commonly dated to August 11, 1973, when DJ Kool Herc spun records at his sister's back-to-school party at 1520 Sedgwick Avenue in the Bronx.

question: Which rapper released the chart-topping 2018 single 'Sicko Mode'?
funFact: 'Sicko Mode' features Drake and famously switches beats three times in a single track, yet still reached No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100.

question: Eminem's song 'Lose Yourself' was written for which movie?
funFact: 'Lose Yourself' was the first rap song ever to win the Academy Award for Best Original Song, but Eminem skipped the ceremony and was asleep at home when it won.

question: What was Kanye West's debut studio album, released in 2004?
funFact: Kanye recorded the album's single 'Through the Wire' while his jaw was still wired shut after a near-fatal 2002 car crash.

question: The duo OutKast comes from which American city?
funFact: OutKast's 'Speakerboxxx/The Love Below' won the 2004 Grammy for Album of the Year, one of the very few hip-hop albums ever to take the top prize.

question: What was Cardi B's breakout 2017 single that topped the Billboard Hot 100?
funFact: With 'Bodak Yellow,' Cardi B became the first solo female rapper to top the Hot 100 since Lauryn Hill's 'Doo Wop (That Thing)' in 1998.

question: Run-D.M.C.'s 1986 hit 'Walk This Way' was a collaboration with which rock band?
funFact: The collaboration reached No. 4 on the Hot 100, broke rap onto MTV in a major way, and is widely credited with reviving Aerosmith's career.

question: Dr. Dre and Suge Knight co-founded which record label in 1991?
funFact: Three decades later, in 2022, Snoop Dogg acquired the Death Row Records brand, the label that launched his own career.

question: Which 2003 album was 50 Cent's major-label debut?
funFact: Before the album's release, 50 Cent survived being shot nine times in 2000, an ordeal that became central to his larger-than-life image.

question: Which rapper released the 2017 hit single 'HUMBLE.'?
funFact: 'HUMBLE.' was Kendrick Lamar's first No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 as a lead artist, and its video won six MTV Video Music Awards.

question: Which female rapper released the hit 'Work It' in 2002?
funFact: In 2023, Missy Elliott became the first female rapper ever inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame.

question: In what year was Nas's classic debut album 'Illmatic' released?
funFact: Nas was only 20 years old when Illmatic earned a rare five-mic rating from The Source magazine.

question: Which producer crafted the gritty signature sound of Wu-Tang Clan's debut album?
funFact: RZA convinced the other eight members to follow his five-year plan, promising that if they gave him total creative control, Wu-Tang would take over the rap industry.

question: Which group recorded 'Rapper's Delight' in 1979, one of the first commercially successful rap records?
choices: Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five
Afrika Bambaataa and the Soulsonic Force
funFact: 'Rapper's Delight' was built on the groove of Chic's 'Good Times,' and Chic's Nile Rodgers threatened legal action until he and Bernard Edwards received songwriting credit.

question: Which group released the socially conscious 1982 classic 'The Message'?
choices: Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five
funFact: 'The Message' was selected for the Library of Congress's National Recording Registry in 2002, part of the registry's very first class of recordings.

question: Before her solo career, Lauryn Hill rose to fame as a member of which group?
funFact: Her solo album 'The Miseducation of Lauryn Hill' became the first hip-hop album to win the Grammy for Album of the Year, in 1999.

question: Which Kendrick Lamar album won the 2018 Pulitzer Prize for Music?
funFact: DAMN. was the first work outside of classical and jazz ever to win the Pulitzer Prize for Music; the board praised its 'vernacular authenticity.'

question: Which rapper wrote the lyrics to Dr. Dre's 1999 hit 'Still D.R.E.'?
funFact: Jay-Z ghostwrote the entire song, including both Dre's and Snoop Dogg's parts, a fact later confirmed by both Dre and Snoop.

question: Who founded Bad Boy Records in 1993?
funFact: Combs launched Bad Boy shortly after being fired from Uptown Records, and within two years the label was a powerhouse behind The Notorious B.I.G.

question: Which 1991 A Tribe Called Quest album is celebrated for fusing jazz and hip-hop?
choices: People's Instinctive Travels and the Paths of Rhythm
funFact: Legendary jazz bassist Ron Carter, who recorded with Miles Davis, played upright bass on the album's track 'Verses from the Abstract.'

question: The legendary diss tracks 'Takeover' and 'Ether' were exchanged between which two rappers?
funFact: The feud ended publicly in 2005 when Jay-Z brought Nas on stage at his 'I Declare War' concert, and Nas later signed with Def Jam while Jay-Z was its president.

question: In what year did Tupac Shakur die after a drive-by shooting in Las Vegas?
funFact: Earlier that same year, Tupac released 'All Eyez on Me,' one of the first double albums in hip-hop history.

question: The influential duo UGK, made up of Bun B and Pimp C, came from which state?
funFact: The Port Arthur, Texas duo reached a massive new audience when Jay-Z featured them on his 2000 hit 'Big Pimpin'.'

question: Which OutKast album contains both 'B.O.B.' and 'Ms. Jackson'?
funFact: 'Ms. Jackson,' OutKast's first No. 1 hit, was inspired by André 3000's relationship with Erykah Badu and addressed to her mother.

question: Jay-Z's 1998 hit 'Hard Knock Life (Ghetto Anthem)' samples a song from which musical?
funFact: The track was produced by Mark the 45 King, who later produced another era-defining sample-driven hit: Eminem's 'Stan.'

question: What was the title of Eric B. & Rakim's groundbreaking 1987 debut album?
funFact: Rakim's complex internal rhyme schemes on the album changed MCing forever, and he is still routinely cited by rappers as the greatest technician of all time.

question: Which rapper released the comeback anthem 'Mama Said Knock You Out' in 1990?
funFact: The song was literally inspired by LL Cool J's grandmother, who told him to 'knock out' the critics calling him washed up; it won him a Grammy in 1992.

question: Which member of N.W.A left the group in 1989 over a royalty dispute?
funFact: Ice Cube's 1991 response track 'No Vaseline,' aimed at his former group, is still regarded as one of the most devastating diss songs ever recorded.

question: The Roots serve as the house band for which late-night television show?
choices: The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon
funFact: The Roots joined Jimmy Fallon's Late Night in 2009 and moved with him to The Tonight Show in 2014, while frontman Black Thought's 2017 ten-minute Funkmaster Flex freestyle went viral.

question: Which Lil Wayne album sold over one million copies in its first week in 2008?
choices: I Am Not a Human Being
funFact: Powered by 'Lollipop' and 'A Milli,' Tha Carter III won the Grammy for Best Rap Album and capped Wayne's legendary mixtape run of the mid-2000s.

question: Which group recorded 'Fight the Power' as the anthem for Spike Lee's film 'Do the Right Thing'?
funFact: Public Enemy frontman Chuck D famously described rap music as 'the Black CNN' for how it broadcast life in communities ignored by mainstream media.

question: Who was the first hip-hop act inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame, in 2007?
choices: Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five
funFact: Run-D.M.C. became the second hip-hop act inducted, following two years later in 2009.

question: What did DJ Kool Herc call his pioneering technique of extending a record's drum break using two copies of the same record?
funFact: The dancers who saved their best moves for Herc's extended breaks became known as break-boys, or b-boys, giving breakdancing its name.

question: Which member of Mobb Deep produced the iconic beat for 'Shook Ones, Pt. II'?
funFact: The haunting piano loop's source remained a mystery for nearly two decades until fans identified it as a sped-up, pitch-shifted snippet of Herbie Hancock's 'Jessica.'

question: Which acclaimed instrumental album did producer J Dilla release just three days before his death in 2006?
funFact: Donuts came out on J Dilla's 32nd birthday, with much of it composed in the hospital as he battled complications from lupus and a rare blood disease.

question: Which group released the laid-back 1993 classic '93 'til Infinity'?
funFact: Souls of Mischief are part of Oakland's Hieroglyphics collective, and the song's title simply referenced the year it dropped — yet it has stayed timeless ever since.

question: In the 1980s 'Bridge Wars,' KRS-One's Boogie Down Productions feuded with which Queensbridge crew?
funFact: The feud erupted when BDP interpreted MC Shan's 'The Bridge' as claiming hip-hop began in Queensbridge, prompting the Bronx rebuttals 'South Bronx' and 'The Bridge Is Over.'

question: MF DOOM formed the duo Madvillain with which producer for the 2004 album 'Madvillainy'?
funFact: An unfinished version of Madvillainy leaked online about a year before release, nearly derailing the project before the duo reworked it into a cult classic.

question: Who won the first-ever Grammy Award for Best Rap Performance in 1989?
choices: DJ Jazzy Jeff & The Fresh Prince
funFact: The duo won for 'Parents Just Don't Understand' but boycotted the ceremony because the new rap category was not part of the televised broadcast.

question: Geto Boys' 1991 classic 'Mind Playing Tricks on Me' is built on a sample from which soul artist?
funFact: The song loops Isaac Hayes's 'Hung Up on My Baby' and became Houston rap's first major national hit, topping Billboard's Hot Rap Singles chart.

question: Whose show-stealing verse on A Tribe Called Quest's posse cut 'Scenario' launched his career as a star?
funFact: Busta Rhymes, then a member of Leaders of the New School, exploded with his 'rawr rawr like a dungeon dragon' verse, which is still ranked among the greatest guest verses ever.
